MELSubItem.Title: CN AA-29854 (Part A or B) Incorporated

ListItem.Para: NO AUTOLAND advisory message is displayed.
Approach minimums do not require its use.
Operating procedures do not require its use.

Note.Para: The NO AUTOLAND advisory message will be displayed. The airplane must also be dispatched using MEL 22-11-07.
If the AUTOFLIGHT FUNCTION status message is also displayed, the airplane must also be dispatched using MEL 22-11-01.

Dispatch is not allowed if any of the following EICAS messages is also displayed: ANTISKID 1 thru ANTISKID 8; BRAKE 1 thru BRAKE 8; BRAKE 1 ACTUATOR thru BRAKE 8 ACTUATOR; SPOILER ACTUATOR; and SPOILERS .

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Make sure Radio Altimeter circuit breakers LRRA L PWR (CE3433115) and LRRA R PWR (CE3433213) are closed prior to dispatch. Opening the circuit breaker for a Radio Altimeter does not render it inoperative. Radio Altimeter circuit breakers are commanded to close during takeoff after the airplane speed exceeds 40kts (AMM DDG 34-33-01).

ListItem.Para: The SGL SOURCE RAD ALT advisory message may be displayed.
Autothrottle automatic flare retard will be disabled. Operate thrust levers manually during landing flare.
If the SGL SOURCE RAD ALT advisory message is displayed, set A/T ARM switches to OFF prior to performing the landing checklist.
If the selected roll mode does not engage, select an alternate roll mode
If autopilot does not engage when selected, fly the airplane manually and attempt to re-engage when the RA indicates above 200 feet.
The autothrottle disconnects at 400 feet and is not able to be re-engaged until the RA indicates above 1,500 feet. Once above 1,500 feet, the autothrottle can be re-engaged and used normally.

OperationNote.Para: RNP AR APCH operations require two Radio Altimeter.
